title: Canonical double-entry patterns
description: The 10 canonical double-entry patterns the module supports. Each pattern documents a business scenario + the exact debit/credit ledger entries the TransactionRecorder will emit. Every pattern preserves the SUM(debits) == SUM(credits) invariant. Consumers reading this catalogue understand exactly what ledger entries their event will produce.

patterns.regulator_notes: The primary revenue-recording flow. Money moved in from a customer paying the invoice. Note: the initial invoice creation debits AR + credits revenue in the invoice module's own transaction (separate movement, not this pattern).

patterns.balance_check: Transaction 1: SUM(debits) = {refund.amount_cents}, SUM(credits) = {refund.amount_cents}, delta = 0. Transaction 2: SUM(debits) = {refund.amount_cents}, SUM(credits) = {refund.amount_cents}, delta = 0.
patterns.regulator_notes: Every refund produces TWO transactions — one for the money flow, one for the revenue reversal. Both reference the same original invoice_payment transaction via source_transaction_id. Regulator can trace: invoice_payment (revenue recognized) → refund_issued (money out) + adjustment (revenue reversed) — net effect: zero revenue + net cash outflow of refund.amount_cents.

patterns.scenario: Customer files chargeback with their bank
patterns.regulator_notes: Distinct from refund_issued because chargebacks are INVOLUNTARY. Revenue is NOT reversed (the underlying invoice remains paid from an accounting perspective — the bank forced a chargeback but the customer's obligation to pay stands). If the tenant later loses the dispute, an additional adjustment records the write-off; if won, the money returns via a reversal (currently manual accounting_adjustment).

patterns.scenario: Customer redeems a coupon on a purchase

ledger_entries.description: Coupon discount recognized as marketing expense
Revenue reduction for the coupon discount

patterns.regulator_notes: Recognizes the coupon as marketing expense + revenue reduction. This transaction fires AT the invoice payment (in the same DB transaction typically). The following invoice_payment transaction then records the full amount that hit cash + the AR. Net effect on revenue: (invoice.subtotal - coupon.discount) — recognized via the two paired transactions.

patterns.scenario: Tenant files + pays tax to a regulator
patterns.regulator_notes: Clears the tax_payable liability. The GL report's tax_payable slice + this transaction's history give regulators the full VAT filing audit trail.

patterns.scenario: Payment gateway charges a processing fee
patterns.regulator_notes: Records the fee as a separate transaction from the associated invoice_payment (for clean fee tracking). Ops teams review gateway_fees over time to negotiate rate reductions.

Distinct from refund_issued — a refund reverses a PAID transaction; a write-off recognizes a NEVER-PAID invoice as loss. Regulators + auditors review write_offs to verify collection efforts were made + to identify uncollectibility patterns.

patterns.scenario: Tenant moves cash from operational cash to a bank deposit
patterns.trigger_event: internal — TransactionRecorder invoked from a support tool or scheduled bank-sweep
patterns.regulator_notes: External banking flow — the transaction records the movement; the actual bank integration happens outside this module. Reversal via bank_withdrawal pattern.

patterns.scenario: Tenant moves cash from bank deposit back to operational cash
patterns.trigger_event: internal — TransactionRecorder invoked from a support tool
patterns.regulator_notes: Mirror of bank_deposit — cash moves back to operational.

patterns.scenario: Tenant converts cash from one currency to another
patterns.trigger_event: internal — TransactionRecorder invoked from a support tool
patterns.balance_check: This is the ONE exception to the single-currency-per-transaction invariant. Debits are in target_currency; credits are in source_currency. TransactionObserver::finalize() balances by base_currency-normalized amounts using exchange_rate_to_base. Rounding differences (typically ≤ 1 cent) recorded as a companion adjustment transaction to preserve exact balance.
patterns.regulator_notes: Rare — explicit FX conversion of cash. Most cross-currency handled implicitly via the transaction.currency + exchange_rate_to_base fields on business transactions.

notes: All 10 patterns preserve the double-entry invariant: SUM(debits) == SUM(credits) per transaction, single-currency-per-transaction (except currency_conversion which normalizes via base_currency).

The refund-issued pattern is the only one that ships as TWO transactions — because refunds simultaneously affect cash AND revenue recognition. Every other business-critical pattern is one transaction.
Every pattern preserves the reference chain — reference_type + reference_id resolve to the originating business entity for the regulator audit trail.

The source_transaction_id + reversal_reason pair appears on refund + chargeback + write-off patterns because each of them is reversing a prior invoice_payment. Manual adjustment reversals use reversal_reason='accounting_adjustment' via POST /transactions/{id}/reverse.

New patterns land here as new modules integrate. Wave 4.5 adds explicit patterns from the payment / refund / chargeback modules; Wave 5+ may add revenue-recognition patterns (deferred_revenue → revenue) + payables patterns (accounts_payable ↔ cash).
